subject: ap-calculus-bc
theme: The Chain Rule, implicit differentiation, and inverse function derivatives extend differentiation beyond simple compositions to handle any function built from basic building blocks.

core_concepts.title: The Chain Rule
core_concepts.detail: For composite functions: $\frac{d}{dx}[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x)) \cdot g'(x)$. Differentiate the outer function, keep the inner function unchanged, then multiply by the derivative of the inner function.

core_concepts.title: Implicit Differentiation
core_concepts.detail: When $y$ is defined implicitly by an equation like $x^2 + y^2 = 25$, differentiate both sides with respect to $x$, treating $y$ as a function of $x$, and apply the Chain Rule to $y$-terms.

core_concepts.title: Derivative of an Inverse Function
core_concepts.detail: If $f$ is one-to-one and differentiable, then $(f^{-1})'(x) = \frac{1}{f'(f^{-1}(x))}$. Find $f^{-1}(x)$ first, then use the formula.

core_concepts.title: Higher-Order Derivatives
core_concepts.detail: The second derivative $f''(x)$ is the derivative of $f'(x)$. It describes acceleration in motion problems and determines concavity of graphs.

core_concepts.title: Implicit Second Derivative
core_concepts.detail: To find $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}$ implicitly, differentiate $\frac{dy}{dx}$ with respect to $x$ again, substituting the known $\frac{dy}{dx}$ expression to eliminate remaining $y'$ terms.

formulas.name: Chain Rule
formulas.description: Differentiate the outer function evaluated at the inner, then multiply by the derivative of the inner function. This is the most frequently used derivative rule on the AP exam.

formulas.name: Inverse Function Derivative
formulas.description: The derivative of the inverse at x equals 1 divided by the derivative of f evaluated at the corresponding point. You need f and f' but not an explicit formula for the inverse.

formulas.name: Derivative of arcsin x
formulas.description: Valid for $-1 < x < 1$. Often combined with Chain Rule when the argument is not simply x.

formulas.name: Derivative of arctan x
formulas.description: Valid for all real x. One of the most common inverse trig derivatives on the AP exam.

exam_tip: For implicit differentiation on FRQs, always apply the Chain Rule when differentiating terms involving $y$ — write $\frac{dy}{dx}$ explicitly at each step. When evaluating $(f^{-1})'(x)$, first find $f^{-1}(x) = a$ from a given point where $f(a) = x$.
